title: 'This is going to be what makes the Earth secure.' How one California company plans to protect us from dangerous asteroids
summary: Exploration Labs plans the first commercial deep space rideshare mission, Apophis EX, to study the potentially hazardous asteroid Apophis. The mission aims to enhance Earth's protection against dangerous asteroids.

title: We love this star projector's incredible displays and at $25 it's never been cheaper
summary: The Fliti Galaxy Projector offers stunning stellar displays at a discounted price of $25, making it an affordable option for room transformation.

title: 'The Martian' becomes real life: Meet 'Spudnik,' the space potato
summary: A real-life potato named 'Spudnik' has been developed for space exploration, resembling the fictional 'Martian' potato. This edible, purple, fuzzy plant is a breakthrough in space agriculture.

title: Trump invited the Artemis 2 moon astronauts to the Oval Office. Here's what happened
summary: President Trump met with the Artemis 2 moon astronauts at the White House, expressing interest in space travel himself. The event highlighted the administration's engagement with NASA's lunar mission.

title: James Webb Space Telescope's strange little red dots may really be 'black hole stars', X-ray data suggests
summary: X-ray data from the James Webb Space Telescope's strange little red dots suggests they might be 'black hole stars'. This discovery could help determine the true nature of these enigmatic objects.

title: Europe's powerful Ariane 6 rocket launches 32 Amazon internet satellites
summary: Europe's Ariane 6 rocket successfully launched 32 Amazon internet satellites. The liftoff took place at 4:57 a.m. EDT on April 30.

title: 'Star Trek: Strange New Worlds' season 4 gets release date and trailer packed with space cowboys, black holes, and a T-rex
summary: Star Trek: Strange New Worlds season 4 has been announced with a new release date and trailer featuring space cowboys, black holes, and a T-rex. The show's producers claim it is their best season yet.

title: The cosmos wears a galactic sombrero | Space photo of the day for April 29, 2026
summary: The Sombrero galaxy is featured as the Space Photo of the Day for April 29, 2026, earning its name due to its distinctive shape. The image highlights the galaxy's unique structure resembling a galactic sombrero.

title: SpaceX rocket debris could slam into the moon: Here's what you need to know
summary: A spent SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket stage from a 2022 launch is projected to crash into the Moon in early August. This marks an unusual case of space debris impacting the lunar surface.

title: I Am Artemis: Ryan Schulte
summary: The Artemis II astronauts completed a 694,481-mile journey around the Moon and back using the Orion spacecraft, which included daily exercise with a flywheel device managed by Ryan Schulte.

title: US-Indian Spacecraft Captures Mexico City Subsidence
summary: A scientist used NISAR mission data to create a map showing land subsidence in Mexico City between October 2025 and January 2026. The images confirm NISAR's performance in monitoring subsidence in a known hotspot.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4873-4878: Welcome to the Atacama Drill Target
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover began a drill campaign at the Atacama site on Mars to study the first Mount Sharp layered-sulfate bedrock since leaving the boxwork terrain. The mission is led by the Curiosity Science Team, including a planetary geologist from the Smithsonian National Air and Space Museum.

title: US-Indian Space Mission Maps Extreme Subsidence in Mexico City
summary: NASA-ISRO's NISAR satellite mapped extreme subsidence in Mexico City using advanced radar technology, demonstrating its ability to track real-time surface changes from orbit without interference from clouds or vegetation.

title: A Gently Glowing Galaxy
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of the barred spiral galaxy IC 486, located 380 million light-years from Earth in the constellation Gemini. The galaxy's ethereal glow is highlighted in the image taken on April 13, 2026.

title: NASA, Boeing Advance Truss-Braced Wing Research in Test
summary: NASA and Boeing have completed wind tunnel testing for a truss-braced wing aircraft design aimed at improving aerodynamic efficiency. The innovative configuration could reduce fuel and operational costs for future airliners.

title: NASA Demonstrates New Prescribed Burn Capability for Spaceport
summary: NASA demonstrated a new prescribed burn capability at Kennedy Space Center in January 2026, separate from rocket launches. The initiative aims to enhance fire management and safety at the spaceport.

title: Council on Environmental Quality Permitting Innovators Program
summary: NASA's Center of Excellence for Collaborative Innovation (CoECI) is assisting the Council on Environmental Quality (CEQ) in implementing crowdsourcing initiatives through its NASA Tournament Lab. The program aims to enhance federal government operations by leveraging external crowdsourced challenges.

title: SpaceX launches Falcon Heavy on Wednesday after 18 month hiatus
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its Falcon Heavy rocket after an 18-month hiatus. This marks a significant return for the heavy-lift vehicle.

title: Artemis III core stage assembly arrives at the Kennedy Space Center
summary: The core stage assembly for NASA's Artemis III mission has arrived at the Kennedy Space Center. This follows the historic Artemis II launch, which sent four astronauts on a lunar flyby mission.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon Heavy returns, Atlas V and Ariane 6 to launch Amazon Leo satellites
summary: SpaceX's Falcon Heavy, ULA's Atlas V, and Arianespace's Ariane 6 are set to launch Amazon's LEO satellites this week from multiple locations. Six launches are planned across Florida, California, and French Guiana.

title: Curiosity Rover uncovers fresh organic clues on Ancient Mars
summary: NASA's Curiosity Rover has discovered new organic clues on Mars, advancing the search for ancient life beyond Earth. The findings were reported by NASASpaceFlight.com.

title: Progress MS-34 to launch from Baikonur for ISS cargo delivery
summary: Progress MS-34 is scheduled to launch from Baikonur to deliver cargo to the International Space Station (ISS), following the CRS NG-24 mission two weeks prior.

title: Starship Progress at the Cape with Hoping to Launch this year
summary: SpaceX is making progress on the Starship Block 3 at Cape Canaveral, aiming for a launch this year. The first flight of Block 3 is anticipated as the company advances toward the milestone.

title: NASA and Axiom react to OIG Report on delays in Next-Generation Spacesuit Program
summary: NASA and Axiom Space have responded to a critical report from NASA's Office of Inspector General highlighting delays in the development of next-generation spacesuits. The report warns of potential setbacks for upcoming lunar missions.

title: Launch Preview: GPS, Progress, and Starlink missions to launch during busy week
summary: Seven space missions, including GPS, Progress, and Starlink launches, are scheduled across four countries using five different launch vehicles during the week of April 20. This marks a busy period for global spaceflight activity.

title: AST SpaceMobile’s BlueBird 7 declared lost following New Glenn launch
summary: AST SpaceMobile's BlueBird 7 satellite was declared lost after a failed launch aboard Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket. This marks a setback for the company's plans to expand its space-based cellular broadband network.

title: New Glenn launches on third mission, successfully reuses booster for the first time
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket successfully completed its third mission and reused a booster for the first time, marking a milestone for the partially reusable launch vehicle.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website provides industry news related to the space sector. The specific article or content is not detailed in the provided input.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet celebrating 50 hallmark achievements over its 50-year history. The publication highlights key milestones and contributions in space exploration and technology.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA’s ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission is preparing a 35-meter parachute for Mars by sterilizing it to avoid microbial contamination. The parachute, the largest ever for a Mars mission, will slow the rover during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing space debris or satellites. This innovation aims to improve the safety and efficiency of space operations.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, remaining on track for a 2027 launch to search for terrestrial planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to share feedback on space sector topics during the SME Forum 2026, held in Paris on June 8-9, 2026, with an online preparatory forum in April-May.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste's first navigation signal has been successfully received. The achievement marks a significant milestone for the navigation system.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has conducted 57 artificial solar eclipses since July 2025, collecting over 250 hours of high-resolution solar corona data. The mission revealed that solar wind structures in the inner corona move 3-4 times faster than previously believed.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, scheduled for 16–17 September at the agency's technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: CATALYST Unveils its Sat Product for City Skylines
summary: CATALYST has introduced a new satellite product designed for urban planning and risk assessment. The tool aims to provide construction firms, insurers, and city authorities with insights into pre-existing conditions and potential risks in urban development.

title: York Space Systems to Acquire ALL.SPACE for $355M
summary: York Space Systems is acquiring ALL.SPACE for $355 million to expand into high-growth markets like communications and military applications. The deal aims to combine technologies to enhance multi-domain capabilities.

title: DPhi’s Second Demo Brings Compute Space to Orbit
summary: DPhi Space successfully hosted Liquid AI's large language model in orbit to analyze an Earth observation image without ground transmission. The demonstration was part of DPhi's second mission, Clustergate-2, launched on a Momentus Vigoride 7 satellite in March.

title: Golden Dome Will Use COTS Parts, Space Execs Say
summary: Golden Dome satellite bus will utilize Commercial Off-The-Shelf (COTS) parts, according to space executives. Apex Space CEO Ian Cinnamon emphasized that it aims to offer no unique differentiators compared to existing commercial satellite buses.

title: Dems Slam Trump Over NSF Board Cuts
summary: Democrats criticized former President Trump for cuts to the National Science Foundation (NSF) board, calling it harmful to science and innovation. The statement was made by Rep. Zoe Lofgren (D-CA) in response to the policy change.

title: Isaacman Continues to Defend Budget Cuts to Congress
summary: Isaacman defended budget cuts to Congress while emphasizing NASA's goal to accelerate the US return to the Moon. The hearing highlighted the agency's top priority amidst financial adjustments.

title: True Anomaly Closes $650M Series D
summary: True Anomaly, a four-year-old space company, raised $650 million in a Series D funding round. The funding follows the company's recent Golden Dome award for its space-based interceptor technology.

title: Meta Secures Overview Energy Space Solar Power Capacity
summary: Meta has secured an agreement to obtain up to 1 GW of power capacity through Overview Energy's planned solar power-beaming satellite system. This deal marks a significant step in leveraging space-based solar power for large-scale energy needs.

title: Pentagon Taps 12 Companies for Golden Dome SBI Tech
summary: The Pentagon has selected 12 companies for the Golden Dome SBI technology program, awarding contracts worth up to $3.2 billion in total. This initiative aims to enhance space-based defense capabilities.

title: Astra Targets Golden Dome With Small Rockets, Says CEO Chris Kemp
summary: Astra's CEO Chris Kemp announced plans to use small rockets for target practice, aiming to drive scale and reduce costs for commercial and government customers. The initiative is part of Astra's strategy to target the 'Golden Dome' market.

title: EarthDaily CEO Don Osborne Previews Upcoming Launch in Thursday Morning Conversation
summary: EarthDaily Analytics is preparing to launch six satellites this quarter, marking a significant milestone as it transitions to commercial service. CEO Don Osborne discussed this upcoming launch in a recent conversation.

title: Department of Air Force Plans Initiative to Hire AI Talent
summary: The U.S. Department of the Air Force (DAF) is launching an initiative to recruit top AI talent, which will impact the government's space strategy.

title: Canada Cancels WildFireSat Constellation Contract With Spire
summary: Canada has canceled its contract with Spire Global to build the WildFireSat constellation for wildfire monitoring. The government remains committed to the space-based wildfire monitoring program despite the cancellation.

title: SpaceX Launches Final ViaSat-3 Satellite, Completing Trio of GEO Satellites
summary: SpaceX successfully launched the final ViaSat-3 satellite, completing Viasat's trio of high-capacity Geostationary Orbit (GEO) satellites using a Falcon Heavy rocket from Kennedy Space Center.

title: Orbcomm Completes $460M Refinancing to Fund Growth
summary: Orbcomm has completed a $460 million refinancing to support growth initiatives and large-scale customer deployments. The refinancing will help expand the company's platform.

title: UPCOMING: Designing Secure Satellite Systems with FIPS 140-3, CNSA 2.0, and PQC
summary: Designing secure satellite systems is becoming critical due to evolving security requirements like CNSA 2.0 and FIPS 140-3, especially for long-term deployments. The challenge lies in maintaining security with limited ability to update systems over decades.

title: True Anomaly Raises $650 Million in New Round to Continue Scaling
summary: True Anomaly secured $650 million in a new funding round to expand its workforce and scale operations to meet growing customer demand. The company aims to enhance its capabilities in the space industry.

title: ST Engineering Signs Singapore Public Safety Deal with HTX
summary: ST Engineering and HTX will co-develop space-based technologies to enhance Singapore's public safety operations through a new space technology program.

title: MaiaSpace CEO Says “Start Later, Run Faster” as Launcher Works Toward First Mission
summary: MaiaSpace is developing a new rocket for European launch services, aiming to enter the market by taking a 'start later, run faster' approach. The company is working toward its first mission, bucking industry trends.

title: Airbus Defence and Space Improves Revenue and Margin in Q1 2026
summary: Airbus Defence and Space reported improved revenue, adjusted EBIT, and order intake in Q1 2026, nearly doubling its order intake compared to the same period last year.
